title: Tandoori Chicken
tagline: Dhaba-style charred drumsticks with a two-stage marinade, roasted in the OTG.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients using skin-on drumsticks.
Most of the fat is in the skin. Remove the skin before marinating to cut ~120 kcal and ~13 g fat per serving — the deep gashes still let the marinade in.
Already lean, high-protein and low-carb. Soya doesn't belong here; if you want more protein per plate, add an extra drumstick or serve with a side of hung-curd raita.
The marinade time (2+ hours) is inactive — total time reflects a short 1 hr + 1-2 hr two-stage marinade.

items: 500 g chicken drumsticks (5-6 pieces), skin on or off
Deep gashes cut to the bone, 2-3 slits per piece
One tendon snipped at the base of each drumstick
2 tsp Kashmiri red chilli powder (for colour)
1 tbsp lemon juice or white vinegar
1 tsp besan (gram flour), if curd is thin
Salt, only a little (already salted in stage 1)
Lemon wedges, to serve on the side

steps.title: Prep the drumsticks
steps.body: Pat 500 g drumsticks completely dry. Cut 2-3 deep gashes to the bone on each (non-negotiable for flavour penetration) and snip the one white tendon at the base of each — it stops the meat pulling up and splitting during the cook.

steps.title: First marinade
steps.body: Rub in 2 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder + 1 tsp salt + 1 tbsp lemon juice + 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste + 1/2 tbsp mustard oil, working it aggressively into the cuts. Rest 45-60 minutes.

steps.title: Mix the second marinade
steps.body: If you have no hung curd, drain 120 g regular curd in a sieve/cloth for 10-15 minutes, OR stir in 1 tsp roasted besan to soak up moisture. Combine it with 1.5 tsp Kashmiri chilli + 1/2 tsp degi mirch + 1 tsp coriander powder + 1/2 tsp cumin powder + 1/2 tsp garam masala + 1/4 tsp black pepper + 1.5 tsp crushed kasuri methi + 1/2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste + 1 tbsp mustard oil + a little salt. It should be a thick paste, not runny.

steps.title: Second marinade
steps.body: Wipe excess liquid off the chicken (don't wash). Coat with the second marinade, massaging into every cut for 2-3 minutes. Rest at room temperature 1-2 hours (or 4-6 hours refrigerated for deeper flavour). Keep it coated, not dripping — excess marinade insulates and burns.

steps.title: Preheat the OTG
steps.body: Preheat the OTG fully to 200°C for 15 minutes (or 240-250°C if your OTG goes higher — then reduce cook time). Set the rack in the middle with a tray below to catch drips. Fan on if available.

steps.title: Main cook
steps.body: Roast at 200°C for 30 minutes total, flipping once at 15 minutes. After the flip, brush lightly with butter or oil (not more marinade). This gets the meat cooked to the bone.

steps.title: Char phase
steps.body: Switch to grill / top heat and cook 8-10 minutes for the tandoor-style char. IMPORTANT: rotate and flip every 3-4 minutes even if the top looks done — OTGs char strongly from the top only, and skipping this leaves one side pale and feeling raw. Look for localised black spots, not uniform browning.

steps.title: Check doneness
steps.body: At the deepest cut near the bone the juices should run clear and the meat should pull back from the bone, no translucent flesh. If unsure, give it +5 minutes at 200°C before serving.

steps.title: Restaurant finish
steps.body: Straight out of the oven, brush with 1 tbsp butter or ghee and sprinkle a tiny pinch of chaat masala. Rest 5 minutes before eating.

steps.title: Serve
steps.body: Serve with lemon wedges on the SIDE — squeeze just before each bite. Don't squeeze lemon over the hot chicken; the acid tightens the meat and softens the crust 5-10 minutes later.

notes: Pan-sear fallback (no OTG): sear the drumsticks in a hot pan to build char, then lower heat, add a splash of water, cover and cook through — but the OTG roast is the authentic method and worth it.
Air-fryer alternative: 200°C, similar timing, flipping partway.
Use less marinade than feels right — if it drips when you lift a piece, it's too much, and it'll steam instead of char.
Lemon lovers: make a lemon-butter glaze (1 tbsp melted butter + 1/2 tsp lemon juice) brushed on AFTER resting — the fat buffers the acid so it doesn't toughen the meat.

Leftover marinade keeps for a quick chicken breast the next day — thin it with 2-3 tbsp curd or 1 tbsp oil first, coat lightly, and roast a whole breast only 16-18 min at 200°C (breast dries out much faster than drumsticks).
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
